rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF FERNANDO EXPOSITO
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #2, Report
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 12/27/23. (No earlier availability.) NB: Notice may not be mailed, served, or published before the filing of the pleading requiring notice. For purposes of this rule, the "filing of the pleading" occurs when the pleading requiring notice appears on the Court's register of actions (and becomes publicly viewable in decedent estate and trust cases). {LRSF 14.14 A.] (1) The second amendment (when no "first" amendment to this second account and report has been filed, only a "declaration" that amended the petition) was not e-filed until 11/20/23. (2) Furthermore, notice was reportedly mailed on 11/10/23, before the second amendment was even filed on 11/14/23 and well before it was e-filed on 11/20/23. (3) Despite Executor's statement in the second amendment that notice would be given of both his declaration filed on 9/27/23 (i.e., the "first" amendment) and this second amendment, the proof of service filed on 11/13/23 showed notice of just the second amendment. (4) Finally, Michael Sixto Garcia's address differs on the service list from the one the executor alleged in his petition filed on 6/8/23 to be Mr. Garcia's. (5) The executor reportedly advanced $24,000.00 to the estate (see Exhibit A, Schedule B-1, of the accounting petition). He repaid himself $25,749.00 (see the third page of Schedule D-1 of the accounting). No explanation has been given for the $1,749.00 overpayment. (6) Where in the accounting is Executor's $3,925 advance to the estate shown? There is no receipt entry dated 5/4/21. (7) If the estate owes past due management fees and some portion of the attorney's costs advanced, the accounting should have a liabilities schedule. (8) Executor's statement on page 2 of his "Declaration" filed on 11/14/23 (also not e-filed until 11/20/23) is confusing. There are no subtotals shown on Schedule D-2 of the accounting showing either $3,810.25 or $2,694.74. Courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues must be received by the assigned Examiner by 12/18/23. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B. (Please do NOT file pleading versions of proofs of service unless a copy of the mandatory Judicial Council form Notice of Hearing is attached. However, since the clearest form of proof of service is on the reverse side of that JC Notice of Hearing, the pleading versions are unnecessary and, in fact contribute unnecessarily to copying and mailing costs charged against the estate. Attorney costs paid during this accounting period totaled $2,200.48. "Attachment Note '9'" to the second amendment shows costs totaling $2,215.27. See issue (7) above if the attorney is entitled to further reimbursements.)

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF TUAN VAN TRAN
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Reconsideration Of Order Granting Petition To Compel Sale Of Property
rulings.Rulings: This tentative ruling is issued by Judge Stephen M. Murphy. Before the court is Co-Administrators Huy Nhat Do and Lam Tran's Motion for Reconsideration of Order Granting Petition to Compel Sale of Property, brought pursuant to Code of Civil Procedure section 1008(a). A motion for reconsideration brought pursuant to Code of Civil Procedure section 1008(a) must be filed "within 10 days after service upon the party of written notice of entry of the order[.]" On September 29, 2023, Petitioner Oanh Thi Duong filed Notice of Entry of Order/Notice of Ruling Filed Directing Sale of Real Property. The Proof of Service therein states service on the Co-Administrators was completed by mail on September 29, 2023. Counsel for the Co-Administrators was served at an Oregon address. Per Code of Civil Procedure section 1013(a), "Service is complete at the time of the deposit, but any period of notice and any right or duty to do any act or make any response within any period [ ] shall be extended [ ] 10 calendar days if either the place of mailing or the place of address is outside the State of California but within the United States[.]" Petitioners subsequently filed Notice of Motion and Motion for Reconsideration on October 25, 2023, twenty-five days after service of Notice of Entry of Order. The instant motion was not filed ""within 10 days after service upon the party of written notice of entry of the order," plus the additional 10 calendar-day extension of the period to respond under section 1013. The Motion for Reconsideration of Order Granting Petition to Compel Sale of Property is DENIED, as procedurally defective under Code of Civil Procedure section 1008(a). The moving party shall lodge with the clerk in Department 204 by the time set for this hearing a proposed order consistent with this tentative ruling as published above. Any party wishing to contest this tentative ruling by appearing at the scheduled hearing must so advise other parties and the courtroom clerk (415-551-3702) no later than 4:00 p.m. on the day before the hearing. To appear by Zoom, go to zoom.us and click "Join Meeting." Enter Meeting ID number 1602254765 and passcode 514879. To appear telephonically through Zoom, call 1-669-900-6833 and follow the prompts. Enter Meeting ID number 1602254765# and passcode 514879#. Parties appearing via Zoom should use their full names in the "Your Name" section. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling CourtCall at 1-888-882-6878 and obtaining an appearance access code for the hearing's scheduled date and time in Department 204.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF EUGENIO S. AMADOR
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #1, Report, Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 01/16/2024, as the following issues (e-mailed to counsel on 09/26/2023 - see also tentative ruling for 10/31/2023 hearing) remain unaddressed: 1) No proposed order has been received or lodged. The caption must begin with "Order for Final Distribution". LRSF 14.35(I)(6)(b). 2) All the information required by CRC 7.250(a) must be provided as to the sale of the estate real property. 3) An allegation must be made as to whether there are outstanding bills due to any third party for preparing this account. LRSF 14.35(I)(6)(a)(iii). 4) No DE-174 form is on file in response to the creditor claim on file. If the claim was withdrawn, please provide a copy. It should be noted that the creditor dismissed the petition, but not the claim. 5) All heirs of the decedent's predeceased spouse who would take 50% of the estate real property under Probate Code section 6402.5(a)(1)-(3) must be identified and receive notice, as the predeceased spouse's interest in the real property passed to the decedent as the surviving joint tenant. See also section 6402.5(f)(3) & (4). A separate petition under section 11700 is generally required before final distribution to determine additional heirs who were not disclosed at the outset of the proceedings. LRSF 14.35(H). 6) A financial account statement verifying the ending cash balance must be filed. LRSF 14.34(D). A declaration re retention of the original statement required by LRSF 14.59(A) must also be filed. 7) The balance of the estate cash on hand exceeds the FDIC limit. A proof that the estate cash is fully insured in observance of the FDIC limit or is otherwise invested in compliance with section 10533(a)(2) must be filed. 8) A specific amount of net cash distributable to each beneficiary as of a date certain (after payment of the attorney's compensation) must be stated. LRSF 14.35(I)(6)(a). 9) Is Elizabeth Amador Manlapaz the same person as Elizabeth Manlapaz Williams? 10) Paragraph 6 of the attorney's declaration states that his hourly rate is $450, but the attorney's compensation for extraordinary services appears to have been calculated at the $350/hour rate. 11) Please provide supporting time records for the attorney's ordinary services. 12) Please describe the circumstances surrounding the execution of the assignment made by each beneficiary, together with facts supporting no involvement of duress, fraud, or undue influence. See section 11604(b) & (c). Note: An appearance may be required on 01/16/2024 if no satisfactory progress is shown to address the issues.

rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition Under Probate Code 850 For Order Determining That Certain Real Property, Located In The Cit
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required, as the following issues (see tentative rulings for past 3 hearings) remain unaddressed since the initial hearing on 06/14/2023: 1) Each occupant of the 1142 Fitzgerald property identified by the declaration filed on 09/01/2023 must be served in the manner required by Probate Code section 851(a)(2) and CCP sections 413.10 et seq. The proofs of service on file show the occupants were served by mail, which is not a manner authorized by the CCP sections applicable to service of process. If the occupants were served under CCP section 415.30, POS-015 forms signed by the occupants (at least 30 days before the continued hearing) must be filed with a proof of service. If the occupants waived formal service, waivers signed by the occupants must be filed. 2) The addresses of Jewell Hall's 3 children must be confirmed by a verified allegation, as paragraph 14 of the petition did not list them. PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING WITH THEIR FULL NAME. Counsel and parties may appear personally or remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF YAN PAL HUI
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required, as the following issues (e-mailed to counsel on 08/02/2023 - see also tentative rulings for past 2 hearings) remain unaddressed since the initial hearing on 08/09/2023: 1) No proposed order has been received or lodged. 2) No DE-147S form is on file. This form must be filed confidentially and conventionally with a pleading cover sheet attached (cannot be e-filed or attached to any e-filed document). 3) An amended DE-147 form must be filed to provide the petitioner's telephone number at item 2 of the acknowledgment of receipt section. 4) Re the DE-121 form: i) Item 1 must specify all names (aka) by which the decedent was known. See the introductory paragraph of the will for two other names used by the decedent. ii) Notice must given to the alternate executors named in the will. 5) Re the proof of publication: All names (aka) by which the decedent was known must be published. See the introductory paragraph of the will for two other names used by the decedent. 6) Item 3d: The petitioner appears to be the sole beneficiary under the will. Please explain why the petitioner is not proceeding with a spousal property petition. Note: If the petitioner intends to make an election under Probate Code section 13502, the election must be filed within 4 months after letters are issued and must include language required by LRSF 14.35(I)(5) ("statement must clearly state that she has been fully informed about the reasons for a probate (rather than, e.g., a Spousal Property Petition) and the consequences thereof, including potential delay and increased fees.") 7) Item 3f(2): The will appears to be self-proving, but the box was not checked. 8) Item 4 must be checked. 9) Item 8 must list the alternate executors named in the will and their address and relationship to the decedent. PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING WITH THEIR FULL NAME. Counsel and parties may appear personally or remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. For a video appearance, go to zoom.us, click "join a meeting" and input meeting ID 160 225 4765 and password 514879. For an audio-only appearance through Zoom, call 1-669-254-5252 and key in meeting ID "160 225 4765#", then participant ID "0#", then password "514879#". (Toll rates may apply).) The Court prefers that counsel and parties appearing by video input their first and last name into the "Your Name" dialogue box. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling 1-888-882-6878. Please review LRSF 14.1(C) for more information.
